reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.review_body:
A school’s real strength lies not in labels, but in the learning environment it offers—one that balances strong academics with co-curricular activities, sports, and platforms that allow children to explore, perform, and express themselves. Exposure through well-designed associations, competitions, and collaborations helps children connect with the outside world, build confidence, and develop life skills that go far beyond textbooks.

Another practical yet often overlooked aspect is distance from home. A nearby school reduces daily fatigue, saves time, and allows children to participate more actively in academics, sports, and enrichment programmes—contributing significantly to their overall well-being.

Schools that offer a global perspective, especially through international curricula aligned with CBSE or State Board frameworks, provide an added advantage. Such alignment ensures academic rigour while nurturing critical thinking, communication skills, and global awareness—preparing learners for both Indian and international pathways.

reviews.author: Gnaneshwar M
reviews.review_body: Teachers are not well trained and not professional. They don`t implement comments mentioned in PTM. This is Christiaan based school where they teach their religion to students.

reviews.author: smita tagore
reviews.review_body: worst experience. one teacher from Saikpet practices black magic. If teachers superstitious what they will teach students. I took my kid out from the school

reviews.author: Hillbilly Cat
reviews.review_body:
2023 Edit-after the recent changes done in the school, everything is messed up (review based on my younger brother who is presently studying here). Firstly, they have abruptly increased the price of their books. The point here is that they are charging, Rs4000 more than the total cost of the books. The books cost Rs5612 in total with full retail price (this is as usual, they never offer discounts on books). Secondly, they have stopped accepting checks and card payment. This is why I try to convince them but no, the cruel lady Miss Shilpa ma'am came up with stupid reasons and denied my payment method. Thirdly, the tuition fee soared sky-high!

Previously, the school was a money-minded bunch, but now it's a total scam.

Despite the quality of education provided, the only problem is the high charge for school supplies and fees, which is not acceptable.

reviews.author: Kartiki Kolapkar
reviews.review_body: When we go for admission for grade 1 , they told about fees structure, and later teacher is telling mam can you afford fees, then we will proceed very rude behavior parents can afford fees then only we are thinking of school ,administration staff is not at all good.
